signals.headline: Germany March 2026 Inflation Confirmed at +2
signals.raw:
Germany March 2026 Inflation Confirmed at +2.7% | YES | HIGH confidence**  
Germany's annual inflation rate rose to +2.7% in March 2026, up from +1.9% in February, driven by sharp energy price increases (+7.2% YoY) amid the Iran war; this is the highest since January 2024 (+2.9%).[1][3][6] Official data from Destatis released April 10 confirms the jump, exceeding prior expectations and prior-month

signals.headline: South Korea March 2026 Inflation Confirmed at 2
signals.raw:
South Korea March 2026 Inflation Confirmed at 2.2% | YES | HIGH confidence**  
South Korea's annual inflation rate increased to 2.2% in March 2026 from 2.0% in prior months, below market forecasts of 2.4%; data from Statistics Korea aligns with Trading Economics reporting.[2][6] This beat-expectations outcome amid global energy volatility could influence Bank of Korea rate bets.[2]
matched_markets: Will South Korea’s 2026 Annual Inflation be between 2.7% and 2.9%?

signals.headline: Germany 2026 GDP Growth Forecast Halved to +0
signals.raw:
Germany 2026 GDP Growth Forecast Halved to +0.6% | YES | MED confidence**  
Leading institutes cut Germany's 2026 GDP growth forecast to +0.6% from prior +1.3%, citing Iran war energy shocks, per KPMG summary of April 2026 reports; 2027 outlook also lowered to +0.9%.[3] Recent amid ongoing conflict, this could move markets on recession risks despite fiscal support.[3]
